Code fragment: public final TableField<BotDisabledChannelsRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a synthetic auto-increment 'id' column as primary key, but already has a unique constraint on 'channel_id' alone (BOT_DISABLED_CHANNELS_CHANNEL_ID_KEY) which could serve as a natural primary key since channel IDs are immutable external identifiers. Consider using 'channel_id' as the primary key instead of adding a redundant surrogate key.

Code fragment: public final TableField<GuildsRecord, Long> ANNOUNCEMENT_CHANNEL_ID = createField(DSL.name("announcement_channel_id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field("'-1'::integer", SQLDataType.BIGINT)), this, "");
Explanation: Using -1 as a default value to represent 'no channel' is a magic value that should be replaced with NULL. Change the column to be nullable and use NULL to represent the absence of an announcement channel.
